faqs.question: How quickly can you buy my house in Gloucester?
faqs.answer: We can complete the purchase of your Gloucester property in as little as seven days, though most sales complete within two to three weeks. The exact timeframe depends on your circumstances and how quickly the legal work can be processed. Whether your property is in Kingsholm, Quedgeley, or Matson, we work with experienced local conveyancers who understand the Gloucester market and can progress matters efficiently. If you need longer to arrange your onward move, we are happy to work to a completion date that suits you. There is no pressure to rush. You set the timeline and we deliver.

faqs.question: Do you charge any fees to buy my Gloucester property?
faqs.answer: No, we do not charge you any fees whatsoever. There are no estate agent commissions, no arrangement fees, and no hidden costs. We also cover your basic legal fees, so the price we agree is the amount you receive on completion day. With estate agents in Gloucester typically charging between one and two per cent commission, selling a property at the city's average of around £240,000 could save you between £2,900 and £5,800 in fees alone. We believe in complete transparency, and the offer we make is exactly what lands in your bank account.

faqs.question: How do you calculate your offer for Gloucester properties?
faqs.answer: We base our offer on the current market value of your property, taking into account recent sold prices in your specific Gloucester neighbourhood, the condition of the property, and local market trends. We use Land Registry data, Rightmove, Zoopla, and our own extensive knowledge of the Gloucester market. Our offers typically come in below full market value because we provide a guaranteed cash sale with speed and certainty. We are always upfront about that trade-off and encourage you to compare our offer against what you might realistically achieve after agent fees, delays, and the risk of fall-throughs on the open market.

faqs.question: What condition does my Gloucester property need to be in?
faqs.answer: Your property can be in any condition at all. We buy houses, flats, and bungalows across Gloucester regardless of their state of repair. Whether your home has damp issues, needs a full renovation, has structural concerns, or is simply in need of cosmetic updating, we will still make you a fair cash offer. We have purchased Victorian terraces in Kingsholm needing complete restoration and modern houses in Quedgeley in pristine condition. Whatever state your property is in, we assess it individually and our offer reflects its current condition.

faqs.question: Can I change my mind after accepting your offer?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely. You are under no obligation at any point until contracts have been exchanged. If you accept our offer and then decide that selling to us is not the right option, simply let us know and we will part ways with no fees, charges, or penalties. Even after solicitors have been instructed, you can withdraw without cost. We cover all legal expenses incurred up to that point. We understand that selling your home is a major decision, and we want you to feel entirely comfortable throughout the process.

faqs.question: Do you buy flats and apartments in Gloucester?
faqs.answer: Yes, we buy all types of property in Gloucester, including purpose-built flats, period conversions, modern apartments, maisonettes, and ex-council flats. Gloucester has flatted accommodation in the city centre, in converted buildings near the docks, and in modern developments across the city. We have extensive experience purchasing leasehold properties and are comfortable dealing with freeholders, management companies, and the particular complexities that can arise with converted and purpose-built blocks. Whether your flat is in a modern development or a Victorian conversion, we are happy to make an offer.

faqs.question: What about Gloucester properties with non-standard construction?
faqs.answer: Non-standard construction is not a problem for us. Gloucester has a significant amount of post-war housing, particularly in Tuffley, Matson, and parts of other estates, that was built using concrete panel, steel frame, or other non-traditional methods. Many mortgage lenders will not lend on these properties, dramatically limiting the buyer pool through traditional channels. We regularly purchase non-standard construction properties and factor the construction type into our offer. As cash buyers, we are not constrained by lender criteria.

faqs.question: Can you buy my Gloucester house if I still have a mortgage?
faqs.answer: Yes, having an outstanding mortgage is no barrier to selling your property to us. The vast majority of homeowners who sell to us still have a mortgage. On completion day, your solicitor uses the sale proceeds to pay off the remaining balance, and the rest comes directly to you. We will need to know your approximate outstanding mortgage balance early on so we can confirm our offer covers it. In the rare situation of negative equity, we can still discuss your options and may be able to help negotiate with your lender.

faqs.question: What if my Gloucester property currently has tenants?
faqs.answer: We are experienced in buying tenanted properties across Gloucester. Whether your tenants are on an assured shorthold tenancy, a periodic tenancy, or even if there are rent arrears, we can still purchase your property. There is no need to serve notice or wait for vacant possession. We buy with tenants in situ and handle the tenancy arrangements after completion. This is particularly helpful for landlords in Gloucester who want to exit the rental market quickly, especially those with properties in Kingsholm or other areas close to the city centre.

faqs.question: How does the legal process work when selling to you?
faqs.answer: Once you accept our offer, we instruct solicitors on both sides to begin conveyancing. We can recommend a solicitor at no cost, or you are welcome to use your own. Your solicitor handles the standard legal checks, prepares the contract pack, and manages the transfer. Because we are cash buyers, there is no mortgage application to wait for, removing one of the biggest causes of delay. We aim to have contracts exchanged within one to two weeks. The whole process is simpler and faster than a traditional sale, but every legal safeguard remains in place to protect you.

faqs.question: Do you buy properties near Gloucester Docks?
faqs.answer: Yes, we buy properties across the Gloucester Docks area and the wider city centre. The docks regeneration has created a mix of converted warehouse apartments, new-build developments, and nearby period properties that benefit from the area's growing appeal. We understand the particular considerations of docks-area properties, including leasehold complexities in converted buildings and the specific appeal of waterside living. Whether your property is a converted warehouse flat or a Victorian house in the surrounding streets, we can make a fair offer.

faqs.question: What areas of Gloucester do you cover?
faqs.answer: We cover the entire city of Gloucester and surrounding areas, including Quedgeley, Barnwood, Hucclecote, Tuffley, Longlevens, Kingsholm, Matson, Podsmead, Linden, Barton, Abbeymead, Abbeydale, Robinswood, and the city centre. We also buy properties across the GL1 through GL4 postcode areas and into surrounding Gloucestershire villages. Simply get in touch and we will arrange a free, no-obligation assessment of your property.

faqs.question: How is selling to you different from using an estate agent in Gloucester?
faqs.answer: The key differences are speed, certainty, and cost. An estate agent in Gloucester will list your property, conduct viewings, and hope to find a buyer, a process that typically takes four to six months with no guarantee of success. Around one in three agreed sales falls through before completion. With us, you receive a guaranteed cash offer within twenty-four hours, and we can complete in as little as a week. You pay no estate agent fees, no advertising costs, and we cover your legal fees. The trade-off is that our offer will be below full market value. For many Gloucester sellers, the savings in time, stress, and fees make our service the better option overall.

faqs.question: Can you help if I am facing repossession on my Gloucester property?
faqs.answer: Yes, we can often help homeowners in Gloucester who are facing repossession. Time is critical in these situations, and our ability to complete quickly can mean the difference between a controlled sale and a forced repossession. If you have received a possession order or your lender has begun court proceedings, contact us immediately. We can work with your solicitor and mortgage lender to complete the sale before the repossession date, allowing you to clear your debt and potentially retain some equity. We have helped numerous Gloucester homeowners in similar situations.

faqs.question: What happens on completion day when you buy my Gloucester property?
faqs.answer: On completion day, your solicitor receives the agreed purchase funds from our solicitor, usually by early afternoon. Once the money has cleared, your solicitor confirms completion. Your mortgage, if you have one, is paid off automatically from the proceeds, and the remaining balance transfers to your bank account, typically arriving the same day or the following morning. You hand over all keys and ownership transfers to us. There is no need for you to be at the property on the day, though you should ensure it is vacated and cleared by the agreed time. It is a straightforward, stress-free process.
